channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Video: Philippine senator flees ICC arrest over role in drug war
item.description: Philippines Senator Ronald Dela Rosa has taken refuge in the country’s parliament, as police sought to detain him.

item.title: Can China counter US sanctions with its trade power?
item.description: From sanctions to rare earths, the economic rivalry between the US and China is intensifying.

item.title: The crisis is Sudan is much worse than what is acknowledged
item.description: Even in Khartoum, where there is no longer fighting, the humanitarian situation is dire.

item.title: Over 370 Afghans killed in Pakistan conflict in first 3 months of 2026: UN
item.description: Fighting between the Taliban and Pakistani forces intensified in February, with many civilians killed in air raids.

item.title: USA’s Johnny Cardoso expected to miss World Cup through ankle injury
item.description: Atletico Madrid midfielder Cardoso is the latest name in the growing list of injured players for tournament cohosts USA.

item.title: At least nine people killed in explosion at Pakistan market
item.description: About 30 people wounded in blast in town of Sarai Naurang, in Lakki Marwat district, northwestern Pakistan.

item.title: Will Messi play? Argentina answer fan queries in 55-man World Cup squad
item.description: Argentina captain is named in the preliminary squad for the FIFA World Cup 2026 but has not confirmed his participation.

item.title: Mass wedding brings brief joy to couples in devastated Gaza
item.description: Palestinian couples gathered for a mass wedding ceremony in Gaza organised amid rubble and displacement.

item.title: Social media becomes a ‘goldmine’ for fraudsters in Jordan
item.description: Jordanians looking for a bargain are being scammed with false promises of cheap gold in a growing trend of online fraud.

item.title: Israeli military kills six in Lebanon, issues more displacement threats
item.description: The deadly air attack comes just days before Israeli and Lebanese officials are due to meet in Washington, DC.

item.title: LIVE: Starmer under pressure as ministers quit, 80 MPs urge him to resign
item.description: The British prime minister is facing a crisis after his Labour Party suffered election losses.

item.title: Zelenskyy says Russia fired over 200 drones at Ukraine as truce expires
item.description: One killed and four others wounded in attacks on Ukraine's Dnipropetrovsk region, local administration chief says.

item.title: France’s Macron announces $27bn investment in Africa at Kenya summit
item.description: Africa Forward summit comes amid France's fading ties with its former African colonies, as Kenya seeks greater trade.

item.title: ‘Whole morning revolves around gas’: Pakistan’s fuel crisis enters kitchens
item.description: The crisis sees women waking up early, cooking quickly, rearranging meals and planning days around the prospect of gas.

item.title: Trump and Xi: The history of encounters between two superpower leaders
item.description: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping have met six times since 2017.

item.title: Gunman arrested after opening fire on busy street near Harvard University
item.description: Gunman arrested after opening fire on busy street near Harvard University

item.title: Starmer defiant after cabinet meeting amid growing pressure to resign
item.description: The UK prime minister refuses to step down as some cabinet members voice their support.

item.title: Mexico cancels plan to cut school year for World Cup after backlash
item.description: Officials agree to keep the school calendar as originally planned, ending on July 15 and resuming on August 31.

item.title: Palestine weekly wrap: Israeli settlers rampage through West Bank villages
item.description: Settlers uproot olive trees, seize land, and displace families, reflecting unchecked violence across the West Bank.

item.title: ‘Potential security risk’: Unpacking the UK’s trust issues with Palantir
item.description: Analysts say it is challenging to track whether Palantir is sticking to the terms of its NHS contract.

item.title: Starving on the front lines: Food supply in crisis as Ukraine fights Russia
item.description: Photos of emaciated Ukrainians reveal conditions suffered by Ukraine, as meagre rations also afflict Russian soldiers.

item.title: Why is Israel allowed to take part in the Eurovision song contest?
item.description: Five countries are boycotting the 70th edition of Eurovision on May 16 in protest over Israel's genocidal war on Gaza.

item.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 74 as Tehran says ready for ‘aggression’
item.description: US-Iran tensions remain high as Tehran warns against 'aggression' and Trump says the ceasefire is on 'life support'.

item.title: Malaysia rescues 23 migrants after boat capsizes, 14 still missing
item.description: Authorities deploy boats, helicopter and surveillance aircraft to search for missing migrants off Pangkor Island.

item.title: Thunder outlasts Lakers in Game 4 to complete NBA playoff sweep
item.description: Reigning champions Oklahoma City Thunder remain undefeated in playoffs and move on to Western Conference finals.
